message:
🐛 Use leaderboard JSON as rewards source instead of GitHub API (#10081)

* 🐛 Use leaderboard JSON as rewards source instead of GitHub API

The Netlify rewards function was making live GitHub Search API calls
that got rate-limited (403/429) when the shared PAT was exhausted,
and also produced different numbers than the leaderboard because it
lacked bonus points and used a different computation path.

Now it fetches the pre-generated leaderboard JSON from
kubestellar.io/data/leaderboard.json (produced daily by GitHub Action),
caches it in Netlify Blobs for 1 hour, and returns the exact same
numbers the public leaderboard shows — with zero GitHub API calls.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🔧 Regenerate rewards.generated.ts to match Go source

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+    bgClass: "bg-gradient-to-r from-yellow-400/30 via-amber-300/30 to-yellow-500/30",
     textClass: "text-yellow-300",
     borderClass: "border-yellow-400/50",
   },

@@ -195,7 +195,11 @@ export interface GitHubRewardsResponse {
   total_points: number
   contributions: GitHubContribution[]
   breakdown: GitHubRewardsBreakdown
+  bonus_points?: number
+  level?: string
+  rank?: number
   cached_at: string
+  leaderboard_generated_at?: string
   from_cache: boolean
 }
